# MMS System Architecture

## Overview

The Metro Integrated Planning System is a high-performance WebGL application for metro fleet planning. It implements a "Semantic Zoom" UX similar to Google Earth, switching between three visualization layers based on camera zoom level.

## Technology Choices

### Why Astro?
- Static site generation for fast initial load
- Islands architecture allows heavy React components as "islands"
- `client:only="react"` skips SSR for R3F compatibility

### Why React 19?
- Latest concurrent features for smooth UI
- Improved hooks performance
- Better Suspense support

### Why React Three Fiber?
- Declarative Three.js API
- React component model for 3D scenes
- Excellent ecosystem (drei, three-stdlib)

### Why Bun?
- Faster package management
- Native workspace support
- Faster dev server startup

# MMS System Architecture

## Overview

The Metro Integrated Planning System is a high-performance WebGL application for metro fleet planning. It implements a "Semantic Zoom" UX similar to Google Earth, switching between three visualization layers based on camera zoom level.

## Technology Choices

### Why Vite?
- Fast HMR (Hot Module Replacement)
- Modern ESM-first architecture
- Excellent TypeScript support
- Simple configuration

### Why React 18?
- Required for BlueprintJS 6.x compatibility
- Concurrent features for smooth UI
- Improved hooks performance
- Better Suspense support

### Why BlueprintJS?
- Enterprise-grade UI components from Palantir
- Consistent design system (Intent colors, Icons)
- Accessible out of the box
- Dark theme support

### Why React Three Fiber?
- Declarative Three.js API
- React component model for 3D scenes
- Excellent ecosystem (drei, three-stdlib)
- R3F 8.x compatible with React 18

### Why Bun?
- Faster package management
- Native workspace support
- Faster dev server startup

# MMS System Architecture

## Overview

The Metro Integrated Planning System is a high-performance WebGL application for metro fleet planning. It implements a "Semantic Zoom" UX similar to Google Earth, switching between three visualization layers based on camera zoom level.

## Technology Choices

### Why Astro?
- Static site generation for fast initial load
- Islands architecture allows heavy React components as "islands"
- `client:only="react"` skips SSR for R3F compatibility

### Why React 19?
- Latest concurrent features for smooth UI
- Improved hooks performance
- Better Suspense support

### Why React Three Fiber?
- Declarative Three.js API
- React component model for 3D scenes
- Excellent ecosystem (drei, three-stdlib)

### Why Bun?
- Faster package management
- Native workspace support
- Faster dev server startup

## Project Structure

```
system/
├── apps/system/              # Main Astro application
│   ├── astro.config.mjs      # Astro configuration
│   └── src/
│       ├── pages/
│       │   └── index.astro   # Entry point
│       ├── components/
│       │   ├── PlanningView.tsx    # Main React component
│       │   ├── TrainList.tsx       # Sidebar with virtual scroll
│       │   └── TimeScrubber.tsx    # Footer minimap
│       └── styles/
│           └── main.css      # Tailwind CSS
│
├── packages/
│   ├── domain/               # Business logic (pure TypeScript)
│   │   └── src/
│   │       ├── entities/
│   │       │   ├── train.ts  # Train interface
│   │       │   └── task.ts   # Task & Conflict interfaces
│   │       ├── generators/
│   │       │   └── mock-data.ts  # Data generation
│   │       └── utils/
│   │           └── time.ts   # Time utilities
│   │
│   ├── gl-engine/            # WebGL/R3F components
│   │   └── src/
│   │       ├── canvas/
│   │       │   └── InfiniteCanvas.tsx  # Main canvas
│   │       ├── camera/
│   │       │   └── CameraRig.tsx       # Camera controls
│   │       ├── layers/
│   │       │   ├── MacroLayer.tsx      # Shader heatmap
│   │       │   ├── MesoLayer.tsx       # InstancedMesh
│   │       │   └── MicroLayer.tsx      # HTML overlay
│   │       ├── shaders/
│   │       │   └── heatmap.glsl        # GLSL shader
│   │       ├── state/
│   │       │   └── world-machine.ts    # State machine
│   │       └── utils/
│   │           └── coords.ts           # Coordinate utils
│   │
│   ├── toolkit/              # State machine & utilities
│   │   └── src/
│   │       └── state-machine/ # Core state management
│   │
│   └── ui/                   # Atomic UI components
│       └── src/
│           └── components/
│               ├── primitives.tsx
│               └── train-list-item.tsx
│
└── design/                   # Visual reference images
    ├── screen-1.png          # Macro view reference
    ├── screen-2.png          # Meso view reference
    └── ...
```

## Data Flow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    User Interaction                          │
│         (Mouse wheel, drag, keyboard, sidebar scroll)        │
└─────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                                  │
                                  ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    State Machine                             │
│               (packages/gl-engine/state)                     │
│                                                              │
│  Slices:                                                     │
│  ├── viewport: { zoom, x, y, lod }                          │
│  ├── selection: { hovered, selected }                       │
│  ├── schedule: { trains, tasks }                            │
│  └── conflicts: { conflicts[] }                             │
└─────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                                  │
                    ┌─────────────┼─────────────┐
                    │             │             │
                    ▼             ▼             ▼
             ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐
             │  Macro   │  │   Meso   │  │  Micro   │
             │  Layer   │  │   Layer  │  │  Layer   │
             │ (shader) │  │(instanced│  │  (HTML)  │
             └──────────┘  └──────────┘  └──────────┘
```

## Key Components

### InfiniteCanvas
- **Purpose**: Orchestrates the three rendering layers
- **Location**: `packages/gl-engine/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx`
- **Dependencies**: CameraRig, MacroLayer, MesoLayer, MicroLayer

### CameraRig
- **Purpose**: Manages orthographic camera with pan/zoom
- **Location**: `packages/gl-engine/src/camera/CameraRig.tsx`
- **Dependencies**: State machine, R3F hooks

### MacroLayer (Zoom < 0.5)
- **Purpose**: Renders 20-year strategic view as shader heatmap
- **Location**: `packages/gl-engine/src/layers/MacroLayer.tsx`
- **Implementation**: 
  - Single PlaneGeometry
  - DataTexture (4096x50) encoding occupancy
  - Fragment shader with Yellow→Orange→Red gradient
- **Performance**: 1 draw call

### MesoLayer (0.5 ≤ Zoom ≤ 2.0)
- **Purpose**: Renders 60k task blocks as instanced mesh
- **Location**: `packages/gl-engine/src/layers/MesoLayer.tsx`
- **Implementation**:
  - InstancedMesh with BoxGeometry
  - InstancedBufferAttribute for colors/transforms
  - Spatial Hash Grid for O(1) picking
- **Performance**: Single draw call for all instances

### MicroLayer (Zoom > 2.0)
- **Purpose**: Renders detailed task cards with HTML
- **Location**: `packages/gl-engine/src/layers/MicroLayer.tsx`
- **Implementation**:
  - drei's Html component with transform
  - Line2 for dependency curves
  - Full Tailwind styling
  - Accessibility (tabIndex, ARIA)

## Key Interfaces

```typescript
interface Train {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  type: 'metro' | 'regional' | 'freight';
}

interface Task {
  id: string;
  trainId: string;
  startDate: Date;
  endDate: Date;
  type: 'maintenance' | 'overhaul' | 'inspection';
  conflict: boolean;
  dependencies: string[];
}

interface Conflict {
  id: string;
  taskIds: [string, string];
  type: 'overlap' | 'resource' | 'dependency';
  severity: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high';
}
```

## Configuration

### Environment Variables
```bash
# No environment variables required for development
# Production may need:
PUBLIC_API_URL=    # If backend is added
```

### Build Configuration
- `astro.config.mjs` - Astro settings
- `tsconfig.base.json` - Shared TypeScript config
- `bunfig.toml` - Bun settings

## Performance Considerations

### GPU Optimization
- MacroLayer: Procedural shader (no geometry overhead)
- MesoLayer: InstancedMesh (single draw call for 60k objects)
- Spatial Hash Grid: O(1) hover detection

### Memory Management
- DataTexture: 4096x50 pixels = ~1MB
- InstancedMesh: 60k instances = ~5MB GPU memory
- Careful dispose() on unmount

### React Optimization
- useMemo for derived data
- useCallback for event handlers
- Stable keys for lists

## Security Considerations

- No backend currently (client-only)
- Mock data generation is deterministic
- No user input validation needed (display only)

lines: -- Static site generation for fast initial load
-- Islands architecture allows heavy React components as "islands"
-- Latest concurrent features for smooth UI

## Project Structure

```
system/
├── apps/
│   └── canvas/                   # Production app (Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS)
│       ├── index.html            # HTML entry
│       ├── vite.config.ts        # Vite configuration
│       ├── package.json
│       └── src/
│           ├── main.tsx          # React entry point
│           ├── App.tsx           # Main component with layout
│           ├── components/       # BlueprintJS UI components
│           │   ├── AppLayout.tsx     # Navbar, Sidebar, Canvas container
│           │   ├── TrainSidebar.tsx  # Train IDs (Card)
│           │   ├── ZoomControls.tsx  # ButtonGroup +/-

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# MMS System Architecture

## Overview

The Metro Integrated Planning System is a high-performance WebGL application for metro fleet planning. It implements a "Semantic Zoom" UX similar to Google Earth, switching between three visualization layers based on camera zoom level.

## Technology Choices

### Why Vite?
- Fast HMR (Hot Module Replacement)
- Modern ESM-first architecture
- Excellent TypeScript support
- Simple configuration

### Why React 18?
- Required for BlueprintJS 6.x compatibility
- Concurrent features for smooth UI
- Improved hooks performance
- Better Suspense support

### Why BlueprintJS?
- Enterprise-grade UI components from Palantir
- Consistent design system (Intent colors, Icons)
- Accessible out of the box
- Dark theme support

### Why React Three Fiber?
- Declarative Three.js API
- React component model for 3D scenes
- Excellent ecosystem (drei, three-stdlib)
- R3F 8.x compatible with React 18

### Why Bun?
- Faster package management
- Native workspace support
- Faster dev server startup

## Project Structure

```
system/
├── apps/system/              # Main Astro application
│   ├── astro.config.mjs      # Astro configuration
│   └── src/
│       ├── pages/
│       │   └── index.astro   # Entry point
│       ├── components/
│       │   ├── PlanningView.tsx    # Main React component
│       │   ├── TrainList.tsx       # Sidebar with virtual scroll
│       │   └── TimeScrubber.tsx    # Footer minimap
│       └── styles/
│           └── main.css      # Tailwind CSS
│
├── packages/
│   ├── domain/               # Business logic (pure TypeScript)
│   │   └── src/
│   │       ├── entities/
│   │       │   ├── train.ts  # Train interface
│   │       │   └── task.ts   # Task & Conflict interfaces
│   │       ├── generators/
│   │       │   └── mock-data.ts  # Data generation
│   │       └── utils/
│   │           └── time.ts   # Time utilities
│   │
│   ├── gl-engine/            # WebGL/R3F components
│   │   └── src/
│   │       ├── canvas/
│   │       │   └── InfiniteCanvas.tsx  # Main canvas
│   │       ├── camera/
│   │       │   └── CameraRig.tsx       # Camera controls
│   │       ├── layers/
│   │       │   ├── MacroLayer.tsx      # Shader heatmap
│   │       │   ├── MesoLayer.tsx       # InstancedMesh
│   │       │   └── MicroLayer.tsx      # HTML overlay
│   │       ├── shaders/
│   │       │   └── heatmap.glsl        # GLSL shader
│   │       ├── state/
│   │       │   └── world-machine.ts    # State machine
│   │       └── utils/
│   │           └── coords.ts           # Coordinate utils
│   │
│   ├── toolkit/              # State machine & utilities
│   │   └── src/
│   │       └── state-machine/ # Core state management
│   │
│   └── ui/                   # Atomic UI components
│       └── src/
│           └── components/
│               ├── primitives.tsx
│               └── train-list-item.tsx
│
└── design/                   # Visual reference images
    ├── screen-1.png          # Macro view reference
    ├── screen-2.png          # Meso view reference
    └── ...
```

## Data Flow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    User Interaction                          │
│         (Mouse wheel, drag, keyboard, sidebar scroll)        │
└─────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                                  │
                                  ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    State Machine                             │
│               (packages/gl-engine/state)                     │
│                                                              │
│  Slices:                                                     │
│  ├── viewport: { zoom, x, y, lod }                          │
│  ├── selection: { hovered, selected }                       │
│  ├── schedule: { trains, tasks }                            │
│  └── conflicts: { conflicts[] }                             │
└─────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                                  │
                    ┌─────────────┼─────────────┐
                    │             │             │
                    ▼             ▼             ▼
             ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐
             │  Macro   │  │   Meso   │  │  Micro   │
             │  Layer   │  │   Layer  │  │  Layer   │
             │ (shader) │  │(instanced│  │  (HTML)  │
             └──────────┘  └──────────┘  └──────────┘
```

## Key Components

### InfiniteCanvas
- **Purpose**: Orchestrates the three rendering layers
- **Location**: `packages/gl-engine/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx`
- **Dependencies**: CameraRig, MacroLayer, MesoLayer, MicroLayer

### CameraRig
- **Purpose**: Manages orthographic camera with pan/zoom
- **Location**: `packages/gl-engine/src/camera/CameraRig.tsx`
- **Dependencies**: State machine, R3F hooks

### MacroLayer (Zoom < 0.5)
- **Purpose**: Renders 20-year strategic view as shader heatmap
- **Location**: `packages/gl-engine/src/layers/MacroLayer.tsx`
- **Implementation**: 
  - Single PlaneGeometry
  - DataTexture (4096x50) encoding occupancy
  - Fragment shader with Yellow→Orange→Red gradient
- **Performance**: 1 draw call

### MesoLayer (0.5 ≤ Zoom ≤ 2.0)
- **Purpose**: Renders 60k task blocks as instanced mesh
- **Location**: `packages/gl-engine/src/layers/MesoLayer.tsx`
- **Implementation**:
  - InstancedMesh with BoxGeometry
  - InstancedBufferAttribute for colors/transforms
  - Spatial Hash Grid for O(1) picking
- **Performance**: Single draw call for all instances

### MicroLayer (Zoom > 2.0)
- **Purpose**: Renders detailed task cards with HTML
- **Location**: `packages/gl-engine/src/layers/MicroLayer.tsx`
- **Implementation**:
  - drei's Html component with transform
  - Line2 for dependency curves
  - Full Tailwind styling
  - Accessibility (tabIndex, ARIA)

## Key Interfaces

```typescript
interface Train {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  type: 'metro' | 'regional' | 'freight';
}

interface Task {
  id: string;
  trainId: string;
  startDate: Date;
  endDate: Date;
  type: 'maintenance' | 'overhaul' | 'inspection';
  conflict: boolean;
  dependencies: string[];
}

interface Conflict {
  id: string;
  taskIds: [string, string];
  type: 'overlap' | 'resource' | 'dependency';
  severity: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high';
}
```

## Configuration

### Environment Variables
```bash
# No environment variables required for development
# Production may need:
PUBLIC_API_URL=    # If backend is added
```

### Build Configuration
- `astro.config.mjs` - Astro settings
- `tsconfig.base.json` - Shared TypeScript config
- `bunfig.toml` - Bun settings

## Performance Considerations

### GPU Optimization
- MacroLayer: Procedural shader (no geometry overhead)
- MesoLayer: InstancedMesh (single draw call for 60k objects)
- Spatial Hash Grid: O(1) hover detection

### Memory Management
- DataTexture: 4096x50 pixels = ~1MB
- InstancedMesh: 60k instances = ~5MB GPU memory
- Careful dispose() on unmount

### React Optimization
- useMemo for derived data
- useCallback for event handlers
- Stable keys for lists

## Security Considerations

- No backend currently (client-only)
- Mock data generation is deterministic
- No user input validation needed (display only)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# MMS System Architecture

## Overview

The Metro Integrated Planning System is a high-performance WebGL application for metro fleet planning. It implements a "Semantic Zoom" UX similar to Google Earth, switching between three visualization layers based on camera zoom level.

## Technology Choices

### Why Vite?
- Fast HMR (Hot Module Replacement)
- Modern ESM-first architecture
- Excellent TypeScript support
- Simple configuration

### Why React 18?
- Required for BlueprintJS 6.x compatibility
- Concurrent features for smooth UI
- Improved hooks performance
- Better Suspense support

### Why BlueprintJS?
- Enterprise-grade UI components from Palantir
- Consistent design system (Intent colors, Icons)
- Accessible out of the box
- Dark theme support

### Why React Three Fiber?
- Declarative Three.js API
- React component model for 3D scenes
- Excellent ecosystem (drei, three-stdlib)
- R3F 8.x compatible with React 18

### Why Bun?
- Faster package management
- Native workspace support
- Faster dev server startup

## Project Structure

```
system/
├── apps/
│   └── canvas/                   # Production app (Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS)
│       ├── index.html            # HTML entry
│       ├── vite.config.ts        # Vite configuration
│       ├── package.json
│       └── src/
│           ├── main.tsx          # React entry point
│           ├── App.tsx           # Main component with layout
│           ├── components/       # BlueprintJS UI components
│           │   ├── AppLayout.tsx     # Navbar, Sidebar, Canvas container
│           │   ├── TrainSidebar.tsx  # Train IDs (Card)
│           │   ├── ZoomControls.tsx  # ButtonGroup +/-
│           │   └── LODIndicator.tsx  # Tag component
│           ├── canvas/           # R3F rendering
│           │   ├── InfiniteCanvas.tsx
│           │   ├── CameraController.tsx
│           │   └── layers/
│           │       ├── MacroLayer.tsx    # Heatmap (InstancedMesh/Shader)
│           │       ├── MesoLayer.tsx     # Task blocks (InstancedMesh)
│           │       └── MicroLayer.tsx    # Detailed cards (Html)
│           ├── data/
│           │   ├── types.ts          # Train, Task interfaces
│           │   └── generator.ts      # Mock data generation
│           └── hooks/
│               └── useViewport.ts    # zoom, pan, LOD state
│
├── packages/
│   └── toolkit/              # State machine & utilities (React 18 || 19)
│       └── src/
│           └── state-machine/ # Core state management
│
├── URGENT_POC/               # Archived POC (reference only)
│   └── canvas.html           # Validated prototype
│
└── design/                   # Visual reference images
    ├── screen-1.png          # Macro view reference
    ├── screen-2.png          # Meso view reference
    └── ...
```

## Data Flow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    User Interaction                          │
│         (Mouse wheel, drag, keyboard, sidebar scroll)        │
└─────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                                  │
                                  ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    State Machine                             │
│               (packages/gl-engine/state)                     │
│                                                              │
│  Slices:                                                     │
│  ├── viewport: { zoom, x, y, lod }                          │
│  ├── selection: { hovered, selected }                       │
│  ├── schedule: { trains, tasks }                            │
│  └── conflicts: { conflicts[] }                             │
└─────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                                  │
                    ┌─────────────┼─────────────┐
                    │             │             │
                    ▼             ▼             ▼
             ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐
             │  Macro   │  │   Meso   │  │  Micro   │
             │  Layer   │  │   Layer  │  │  Layer   │
             │ (shader) │  │(instanced│  │  (HTML)  │
             └──────────┘  └──────────┘  └──────────┘
```

## Key Components

### InfiniteCanvas
- **Purpose**: Orchestrates the three rendering layers
- **Location**: `packages/gl-engine/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx`
- **Dependencies**: CameraRig, MacroLayer, MesoLayer, MicroLayer

### CameraRig
- **Purpose**: Manages orthographic camera with pan/zoom
- **Location**: `packages/gl-engine/src/camera/CameraRig.tsx`
- **Dependencies**: State machine, R3F hooks

### MacroLayer (Zoom < 0.5)
- **Purpose**: Renders 20-year strategic view as shader heatmap
- **Location**: `packages/gl-engine/src/layers/MacroLayer.tsx`
- **Implementation**: 
  - Single PlaneGeometry
  - DataTexture (4096x50) encoding occupancy
  - Fragment shader with Yellow→Orange→Red gradient
- **Performance**: 1 draw call

### MesoLayer (0.5 ≤ Zoom ≤ 2.0)
- **Purpose**: Renders 60k task blocks as instanced mesh
- **Location**: `packages/gl-engine/src/layers/MesoLayer.tsx`
- **Implementation**:
  - InstancedMesh with BoxGeometry
  - InstancedBufferAttribute for colors/transforms
  - Spatial Hash Grid for O(1) picking
- **Performance**: Single draw call for all instances

### MicroLayer (Zoom > 2.0)
- **Purpose**: Renders detailed task cards with HTML
- **Location**: `packages/gl-engine/src/layers/MicroLayer.tsx`
- **Implementation**:
  - drei's Html component with transform
  - Line2 for dependency curves
  - Full Tailwind styling
  - Accessibility (tabIndex, ARIA)

## Key Interfaces

```typescript
interface Train {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  type: 'metro' | 'regional' | 'freight';
}

interface Task {
  id: string;
  trainId: string;
  startDate: Date;
  endDate: Date;
  type: 'maintenance' | 'overhaul' | 'inspection';
  conflict: boolean;
  dependencies: string[];
}

interface Conflict {
  id: string;
  taskIds: [string, string];
  type: 'overlap' | 'resource' | 'dependency';
  severity: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high';
}
```

## Configuration

### Environment Variables
```bash
# No environment variables required for development
# Production may need:
PUBLIC_API_URL=    # If backend is added
```

### Build Configuration
- `astro.config.mjs` - Astro settings
- `tsconfig.base.json` - Shared TypeScript config
- `bunfig.toml` - Bun settings

## Performance Considerations

### GPU Optimization
- MacroLayer: Procedural shader (no geometry overhead)
- MesoLayer: InstancedMesh (single draw call for 60k objects)
- Spatial Hash Grid: O(1) hover detection

### Memory Management
- DataTexture: 4096x50 pixels = ~1MB
- InstancedMesh: 60k instances = ~5MB GPU memory
- Careful dispose() on unmount

### React Optimization
- useMemo for derived data
- useCallback for event handlers
- Stable keys for lists

## Security Considerations

- No backend currently (client-only)
- Mock data generation is deterministic
- No user input validation needed (display only)

# MMS System Architecture

## Overview

The Metro Integrated Planning System is a high-performance WebGL application for metro fleet planning. It implements a "Semantic Zoom" UX similar to Google Earth, switching between three visualization layers based on camera zoom level.

## Technology Choices

### Why Vite?
- Fast HMR (Hot Module Replacement)
- Modern ESM-first architecture
- Excellent TypeScript support
- Simple configuration

### Why React 18?
- Required for BlueprintJS 6.x compatibility
- Concurrent features for smooth UI
- Improved hooks performance
- Better Suspense support

### Why BlueprintJS?
- Enterprise-grade UI components from Palantir
- Consistent design system (Intent colors, Icons)
- Accessible out of the box
- Dark theme support

### Why React Three Fiber?
- Declarative Three.js API
- React component model for 3D scenes
- Excellent ecosystem (drei, three-stdlib)
- R3F 8.x compatible with React 18

### Why Bun?
- Faster package management
- Native workspace support
- Faster dev server startup

## Project Structure

```
system/
├── apps/
│   └── canvas/                   # Production app (Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS)
│       ├── index.html            # HTML entry
│       ├── vite.config.ts        # Vite configuration
│       ├── package.json
│       └── src/
│           ├── main.tsx          # React entry point
│           ├── App.tsx           # Main component with layout
│           ├── components/       # BlueprintJS UI components
│           │   ├── AppLayout.tsx     # Navbar, Sidebar, Canvas container
│           │   ├── TrainSidebar.tsx  # Train IDs (Card)
│           │   ├── ZoomControls.tsx  # ButtonGroup +/-
│           │   └── LODIndicator.tsx  # Tag component
│           ├── canvas/           # R3F rendering
│           │   ├── InfiniteCanvas.tsx
│           │   ├── CameraController.tsx
│           │   └── layers/
│           │       ├── MacroLayer.tsx    # Heatmap (InstancedMesh/Shader)
│           │       ├── MesoLayer.tsx     # Task blocks (InstancedMesh)
│           │       └── MicroLayer.tsx    # Detailed cards (Html)
│           ├── data/
│           │   ├── types.ts          # Train, Task interfaces
│           │   └── generator.ts      # Mock data generation
│           └── hooks/
│               └── useViewport.ts    # zoom, pan, LOD state
│
├── packages/
│   └── toolkit/              # State machine & utilities (React 18 || 19)
│       └── src/
│           └── state-machine/ # Core state management
│
├── URGENT_POC/               # Archived POC (reference only)
│   └── canvas.html           # Validated prototype
│
└── design/                   # Visual reference images
    ├── screen-1.png          # Macro view reference
    ├── screen-2.png          # Meso view reference
    └── ...
```

## Data Flow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    User Interaction                          │
│         (Mouse wheel, drag, keyboard, sidebar scroll)        │
└─────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                                  │
                                  ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    State Machine                             │
│               (packages/gl-engine/state)                     │
│                                                              │
│  Slices:                                                     │
│  ├── viewport: { zoom, x, y, lod }                          │
│  ├── selection: { hovered, selected }                       │
│  ├── schedule: { trains, tasks }                            │
│  └── conflicts: { conflicts[] }                             │
└─────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                                  │
                    ┌─────────────┼─────────────┐
                    │             │             │
                    ▼             ▼             ▼
             ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐
             │  Macro   │  │   Meso   │  │  Micro   │
             │  Layer   │  │   Layer  │  │  Layer   │
             │ (shader) │  │(instanced│  │  (HTML)  │
             └──────────┘  └──────────┘  └──────────┘
```

## Key Components

### InfiniteCanvas
- **Purpose**: Orchestrates the three rendering layers
- **Location**: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx`
- **Dependencies**: CameraController, MacroLayer, MesoLayer, MicroLayer

### CameraController
- **Purpose**: Manages orthographic camera with pan/zoom
- **Location**: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/CameraController.tsx`
- **Dependencies**: useViewport hook, R3F hooks (useThree, useFrame)

### MacroLayer (Zoom < 0.5)
- **Purpose**: Renders 20-year strategic view as heatmap
- **Location**: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MacroLayer.tsx`
- **Implementation**:
  - InstancedMesh with colored cells
  - Or: ShaderMaterial with DataTexture
  - Yellow→Orange→Red gradient based on task density
- **Performance**: Minimal draw calls

### MesoLayer (0.5 ≤ Zoom ≤ 2.0)
- **Purpose**: Renders task blocks as instanced mesh
- **Location**: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MesoLayer.tsx`
- **Implementation**:
  - InstancedMesh with BoxGeometry
  - InstancedBufferAttribute for colors/transforms

- **Performance**: Single draw call for all instances

### MicroLayer (Zoom > 2.0)
- **Purpose**: Renders detailed task cards with HTML

- **Implementation**:
  - drei's Html component with transform
  - BlueprintJS styling (Card, Tag)
  - Accessibility (tabIndex, ARIA)

## Key Interfaces

```typescript
interface Train {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  type: 'metro' | 'regional' | 'freight';
}

interface Task {
  id: string;
  trainId: string;
  startDate: Date;
  endDate: Date;
  type: 'maintenance' | 'overhaul' | 'inspection';
  conflict: boolean;
  dependencies: string[];
}

interface Conflict {
  id: string;
  taskIds: [string, string];
  type: 'overlap' | 'resource' | 'dependency';
  severity: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high';
}
```

## Configuration

### Environment Variables
```bash
# No environment variables required for development
# Production may need:
PUBLIC_API_URL=    # If backend is added
```

### Build Configuration
- `astro.config.mjs` - Astro settings
- `tsconfig.base.json` - Shared TypeScript config
- `bunfig.toml` - Bun settings

## Performance Considerations

### GPU Optimization
- MacroLayer: Procedural shader (no geometry overhead)
- MesoLayer: InstancedMesh (single draw call for 60k objects)
- Spatial Hash Grid: O(1) hover detection

### Memory Management
- DataTexture: 4096x50 pixels = ~1MB
- InstancedMesh: 60k instances = ~5MB GPU memory
- Careful dispose() on unmount

### React Optimization
- useMemo for derived data
- useCallback for event handlers
- Stable keys for lists

## Security Considerations

- No backend currently (client-only)
- Mock data generation is deterministic
- No user input validation needed (display only)

# MMS System Architecture

## Overview

The Metro Integrated Planning System is a high-performance WebGL application for metro fleet planning. It implements a "Semantic Zoom" UX similar to Google Earth, switching between three visualization layers based on camera zoom level.

## Technology Choices

### Why Vite?
- Fast HMR (Hot Module Replacement)
- Modern ESM-first architecture
- Excellent TypeScript support
- Simple configuration

### Why React 18?
- Required for BlueprintJS 6.x compatibility
- Concurrent features for smooth UI
- Improved hooks performance
- Better Suspense support

### Why BlueprintJS?
- Enterprise-grade UI components from Palantir
- Consistent design system (Intent colors, Icons)
- Accessible out of the box
- Dark theme support

### Why React Three Fiber?
- Declarative Three.js API
- React component model for 3D scenes
- Excellent ecosystem (drei, three-stdlib)
- R3F 8.x compatible with React 18

### Why Bun?
- Faster package management
- Native workspace support
- Faster dev server startup

├── apps/

- Spatial Hash Grid: O(1) hover detection

### Memory Management
- DataTexture: 4096x50 pixels = ~1MB
- InstancedMesh: 60k instances = ~5MB GPU memory
- Careful dispose() on unmount

### React Optimization
- useMemo for derived data
- useCallback for event handlers
- Stable keys for lists

## Security Considerations

- No backend currently (client-only)
- Mock data generation is deterministic
- No user input validation needed (display only)

# MMS System Architecture

## Overview

The Metro Integrated Planning System is a high-performance WebGL application for metro fleet planning. It implements a "Semantic Zoom" UX similar to Google Earth, switching between three visualization layers based on camera zoom level.

## Technology Choices

### Why Vite?
- Fast HMR (Hot Module Replacement)
- Modern ESM-first architecture
- Excellent TypeScript support
- Simple configuration

### Why React 18?
- Required for BlueprintJS 6.x compatibility
- Concurrent features for smooth UI
- Improved hooks performance
- Better Suspense support

### Why BlueprintJS?
- Enterprise-grade UI components from Palantir
- Consistent design system (Intent colors, Icons)
- Accessible out of the box
- Dark theme support

### Why React Three Fiber?
- Declarative Three.js API
- React component model for 3D scenes
- Excellent ecosystem (drei, three-stdlib)

**Version Pairing (Critical):**
- React 18 → `@react-three/fiber@8.x` + `@react-three/drei@9.x`
- React 19 → `@react-three/fiber@9.x` + `@react-three/drei@10.x`

Since BlueprintJS 6 requires React 18, we use R3F 8.x.

### Why Bun?
- Faster package management
- Native workspace support
- Faster dev server startup

## Project Structure

```
system/
├── apps/
│   └── canvas/                   # Production app (Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS)
│       ├── index.html            # HTML entry
│       ├── vite.config.ts        # Vite configuration
│       ├── package.json
│       └── src/
│           ├── main.tsx          # React entry point
│           ├── App.tsx           # Main component with layout
│           ├── components/       # BlueprintJS UI components
│           │   ├── AppLayout.tsx     # Navbar, Sidebar, Canvas container
│           │   ├── TrainSidebar.tsx  # Train IDs (Card)
│           │   ├── ZoomControls.tsx  # ButtonGroup +/-
│           │   └── LODIndicator.tsx  # Tag component
│           ├── canvas/           # R3F rendering
│           │   ├── InfiniteCanvas.tsx
│           │   ├── CameraController.tsx
│           │   └── layers/
│           │       ├── MacroLayer.tsx    # Heatmap (InstancedMesh/Shader)
│           │       ├── MesoLayer.tsx     # Task blocks (InstancedMesh)
│           │       └── MicroLayer.tsx    # Detailed cards (Html)
│           ├── data/
│           │   ├── types.ts          # Train, Task interfaces
│           │   └── generator.ts      # Mock data generation
│           └── hooks/
│               └── useViewport.ts    # zoom, pan, LOD state
│
├── packages/
│   └── toolkit/              # State machine & utilities (React 18 || 19)
│       └── src/
│           └── state-machine/ # Core state management
│
├── URGENT_POC/               # Archived POC (reference only)
│   └── canvas.html           # Validated prototype
│
└── design/                   # Visual reference images
    ├── screen-1.png          # Macro view reference
    ├── screen-2.png          # Meso view reference
    └── ...
```

## Data Flow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    User Interaction                          │
│         (Mouse wheel, drag, keyboard, sidebar scroll)        │
└─────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                                  │
                                  ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    State Machine                             │
│               (packages/gl-engine/state)                     │
│                                                              │
│  Slices:                                                     │
│  ├── viewport: { zoom, x, y, lod }                          │
│  ├── selection: { hovered, selected }                       │
│  ├── schedule: { trains, tasks }                            │
│  └── conflicts: { conflicts[] }                             │
└─────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                                  │
                    ┌─────────────┼─────────────┐
                    │             │             │
                    ▼             ▼             ▼
             ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐
             │  Macro   │  │   Meso   │  │  Micro   │
             │  Layer   │  │   Layer  │  │  Layer   │
             │ (shader) │  │(instanced│  │  (HTML)  │
             └──────────┘  └──────────┘  └──────────┘
```

## Key Components

### InfiniteCanvas
- **Purpose**: Orchestrates the three rendering layers
- **Location**: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx`
- **Dependencies**: CameraController, MacroLayer, MesoLayer, MicroLayer

### CameraController
- **Purpose**: Manages orthographic camera with pan/zoom
- **Location**: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/CameraController.tsx`
- **Dependencies**: useViewport hook, R3F hooks (useThree, useFrame)

### MacroLayer (Zoom < 0.5)
- **Purpose**: Renders 20-year strategic view as heatmap
- **Location**: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MacroLayer.tsx`
- **Implementation**:
  - InstancedMesh with colored cells
  - Or: ShaderMaterial with DataTexture
  - Yellow→Orange→Red gradient based on task density
- **Performance**: Minimal draw calls

### MesoLayer (0.5 ≤ Zoom ≤ 2.0)
- **Purpose**: Renders task blocks as instanced mesh
- **Location**: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MesoLayer.tsx`
- **Implementation**:
  - InstancedMesh with BoxGeometry
  - InstancedBufferAttribute for colors/transforms
  - Conflict tasks: Intent.DANGER red (#cd4246)
- **Performance**: Single draw call for all instances

### MicroLayer (Zoom > 2.0)
- **Purpose**: Renders detailed task cards with HTML
- **Location**: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MicroLayer.tsx`
- **Implementation**:
  - drei's Html component with transform
  - BlueprintJS styling (Card, Tag)
  - Accessibility (tabIndex, ARIA)

## Key Interfaces

```typescript
interface Train {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  type: 'metro' | 'regional' | 'freight';
}

interface Task {
  id: string;
  trainId: string;
  startDate: Date;
  endDate: Date;
  type: 'maintenance' | 'overhaul' | 'inspection';
  conflict: boolean;
  dependencies: string[];
}

interface Conflict {
  id: string;
  taskIds: [string, string];
  type: 'overlap' | 'resource' | 'dependency';
  severity: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high';
}
```

## Configuration

### Environment Variables
```bash
# No environment variables required for development
# Production may need:
VITE_API_URL=    # If backend is added
```

### Build Configuration
- `apps/canvas/vite.config.ts` - Vite settings
- `apps/canvas/tsconfig.json` - TypeScript config
- `bunfig.toml` - Bun workspace settings

## Performance Considerations

### GPU Optimization
- MacroLayer: Procedural shader (no geometry overhead)
- MesoLayer: InstancedMesh (single draw call for 60k objects)
- Spatial Hash Grid: O(1) hover detection

### Memory Management
- DataTexture: 4096x50 pixels = ~1MB
- InstancedMesh: 60k instances = ~5MB GPU memory
- Careful dispose() on unmount

### React Optimization
- useMemo for derived data
- useCallback for event handlers
- Stable keys for lists

## Security Considerations

- No backend currently (client-only)
- Mock data generation is deterministic
- No user input validation needed (display only)

# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Harness Consolidation)

### Documentation Update - canvas.html as Single Product

**Changes:**
- Redesigned `features.json` with canvas.html as the single product entry point
- Consolidated all features to target canvas.html (F001-F028)
- Updated `CLAUDE.md` URGENT_POC section to reflect canvas.html focus
- Updated `docs/UX_SPECIFICATION.md` implementation details
- Updated `docs/README.md` with current implementation status

**New Feature Structure:**
- F001-F015: Core canvas features (PASS) - pan, zoom, LOD, sidebar, timeline
- F016-F017: Scroll modifiers (PENDING) - Shift+Scroll, Ctrl+Scroll
- F018-F020: Interactions (PENDING) - hover, click, tooltip
- F021-F022: Accessibility (PENDING) - keyboard nav, ARIA
- F023-F028: Advanced features (PENDING) - dependencies, minimap, shortcuts

**Archive:**
- Legacy HTML files (index.html, macro.html, meso.html, micro.html, layout.html) kept as visual references only
- These files are NOT the product - canvas.html is the single entry point

**Rationale:**
- The semantic zoom UX requires a single interactive canvas
- Multiple static HTML files were prototypes to validate individual layer designs
- canvas.html integrates all layers with real pan/zoom interaction

---

## Session: 2025-12-15 (F017 Pan/Zoom Interactivity)

### F017: Pan/Zoom Interactivity - COMPLETE

**Implemented:**
- Created `URGENT_POC/canvas.html` with full semantic zoom
- Drag-to-pan: Click and drag to move the viewport
- Scroll-to-zoom: Mouse wheel zooms toward cursor position
- Double-click to zoom in 2x at click location
- Zoom controls (+/- buttons) and Reset View button
- LOD transitions working correctly:
  - **MACRO** (zoom < 0.5x): Heatmap with yellow-orange-red gradient
  - **MESO** (zoom 0.5-2x): DNA strips with colored task blocks
  - **MICRO** (zoom > 2x): Detailed task cards with labels and grid
- Timeline header adapts granularity (years → months → weeks → days)
- Sidebar shows visible train IDs synced with viewport
- LOD indicator in header shows current zoom level

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**

## Session: 2025-12-15 (F016-BUG Verification)

### F016-BUG: layout.html JS rendering issue - RESOLVED

**Investigation:**
- Visual test via Playwright MCP shows page renders correctly
- Timeline header (Oct 1-8) displays properly

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Claude Code Hooks)

### Implemented Harness Enforcement via Hooks

Based on:
- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

**Hooks Created:**
- [x] `session-start.sh` (SessionStart) - Harness protocol reminders + package versions
- [x] `protect-package-json.sh` (PreToolUse) - BLOCKS manual package.json edits
- [x] `after-write.sh` (PostToolUse) - Quick feedback after file edits
- [x] `task-type-detector.py` (UserPromptSubmit) - Task-type aware reminders
- [x] `end-of-turn-check.sh` (Stop) - Quality gate at end of turn

**Architecture:**
```
PreToolUse  → BLOCKING (prevent bad actions)
PostToolUse → FAST (quick feedback)
Stop        → QUALITY GATE (typecheck, visual test reminders)
```

## Recurring Issues & Solutions

### Issue: R3F or Three.js import errors
**Solution**: Use `bun add` to install packages, never edit package.json manually

### Issue: Astro hydration issues with React
**Solution**: Always use `client:only="react"` for R3F components

### Issue: Shader compilation errors
**Solution**: Check GLSL syntax, verify uniform names match

### Issue: TypeScript errors with toolkit state machine
**Solution**: Use usePlanningSelector() instead of usePlanning() for type safety

All tests pass:
- ✅ App runs on localhost:5173 without errors
- ✅ BlueprintJS Navbar renders correctly
- ✅ Zoom indicator shows current zoom level
- ✅ LOD Tag changes color based on zoom:
  - MACRO (yellow/warning) at < 0.5x
  - MESO (blue/primary) at 0.5-2x
  - MICRO (green/success) at > 2x
- ✅ Zoom buttons work (+/- and Reset)
- ✅ Sidebar shows train IDs
- ✅ No console errors

F101 is complete. Let me close the browser, update the features.json to mark F101 as passes:true, update the todo list, and commit.

{
  "project": "Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)",
  "version": "0.2.0",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-15",
  "product": {
    "entry": "apps/canvas",
    "description": "Production React app with semantic zoom (Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6)"
  },
  "poc": {
    "entry": "URGENT_POC/canvas.html",
    "description": "Archived POC - validated prototype, migrating to production app",
    "status": "archived"
  },
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "F001",
      "category": "core",
      "description": "Canvas renders and initializes without errors",
      "steps": [
        "Open URGENT_POC/canvas.html in browser",
        "Canvas displays with header, sidebar, and main area",
        "No JavaScript errors in console",
        "Mock data generates 50 trains with 10 years of tasks"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "Foundation - canvas.html is the single product entry point"
    },
    {
      "id": "F002",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Pan navigation with drag",
      "steps": [
        "Click and drag on canvas to pan",
        "Cursor changes to grabbing while dragging",
        "Pan works in all directions (X and Y)",
        "Sidebar and timeline header sync with pan position"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Implemented with mousedown/mousemove/mouseup events"
    },
    {
      "id": "F003",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Zoom navigation with scroll wheel",
      "steps": [
        "Scroll up to zoom in, scroll down to zoom out",
        "Zoom centers on mouse cursor position",
        "Zoom respects MIN_ZOOM (0.1x) and MAX_ZOOM (5x) limits",
        "Zoom level indicator updates in header"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Zoom toward mouse position for natural feel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F004",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Double-click zoom",
      "steps": [
        "Double-click zooms in 2x at click location",
        "Works at any current zoom level (respects max)",
        "Animation is smooth (no jarring jump)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Quick zoom in gesture"
    },
    {
      "id": "F005",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Zoom button controls",
      "steps": [
        "Plus button zooms in by 1.5x",
        "Minus button zooms out by 1.5x",
        "Reset button returns to zoom 1.0 and pan origin",
        "Buttons zoom toward center of viewport"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Alternative for users without scroll wheel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F006",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "Macro layer renders at zoom < 0.5x",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom out below 0.5x to trigger Macro view",
        "Heatmap displays with Yellow-Orange-Red gradient",
        "Cell density reflects task count per month/train",
